task_completion.description: Agent accesses the target developer platforms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a web browser navigated to github.com, gitlab.com, bitbucket.org, npmjs.com, and/or pypi.org, or terminal windows showing curl/wget requests to these domains.

task_completion.description: Agent inspects the HTTP response headers for the target sites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the browser's Developer Tools open to the Network tab with the main document request selected and 'Response Headers' visible, or terminal output showing HTTP headers (e.g., from curl -I).

task_completion.description: Agent creates the required JSON audit report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (like VS Code, nano, or vim) editing a file named 'security_audit_report.json' in the '~/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Agent formats the JSON with top-level keys for each site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the JSON file showing top-level keys exactly matching the domain names: 'github.com', 'gitlab.com', 'bitbucket.org', 'npmjs.com', and 'pypi.org'.

task_completion.description: Agent records at least 3 required security headers per site with their actual string values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the JSON file showing keys like 'Strict-Transport-Security', 'Content-Security-Policy', 'X-Content-Type-Options', or 'X-Frame-Options' nested under the domains, populated with real values (e.g., 'nosniff', 'max-age=31536000', 'deny').

integrity.description: Agent actually inspected the headers rather than hallucinating them from training data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual usage of Browser Developer Tools (Network tab) or terminal network commands (curl/wget) fetching the live headers before writing them to the JSON file.

integrity.description: Agent recorded genuine, complex header values rather than placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON values should contain realistic directives (e.g., 'max-age=31536000; includeSubdomains; preload' for STS, or 'nosniff' for XCTO) rather than simple 'yes', 'true', or placeholder text.

integrity.description: Agent saved the file in the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal commands must show the file being saved specifically to '/home/ga/Documents/security_audit_report.json' or '~/Documents/security_audit_report.json'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target sites: github.com, gitlab.com, bitbucket.org, npmjs.com, pypi.org. Required headers: Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options. Output file: ~/Documents/security_audit_report.json. Actual values to verify against: X-Content-Type-Options is 'nosniff' for all 5 sites. Strict-Transport-Security contains 'max-age=31536000' or 'max-age=63072000'. X-Frame-Options is typically 'deny' or 'SAMEORIGIN'. 4 out of 5 sites have all 4 headers; npmjs.com only has 2 of the required headers, so the agent may record empty strings, nulls, or omit the missing headers for npmjs.com to meet the '3 headers per site' formatting requirement.
